subtopic: Patterns that emerged after the 2023 Yan/Weng baseline
note: This entry catalogs patterns that did not exist or were not widespread when Yan (Jul 2023) and Weng (Jun 2023) wrote. Each item should be re-verified before relied on. Anchored sources are listed; individual facts are NOT individually verified by fetch.

patterns.name: Reasoning models / test-time compute scaling
patterns.summary: Models trained (often via RL on verifiable rewards) to produce long internal chain-of-thought before answering. Quality scales with thinking-time budget, not just parameter count.
patterns.implication: Some problems are now better solved by giving one big model more think-time than by orchestrating many agents.

patterns.name: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
patterns.summary: Open standard from Anthropic for connecting LLMs to tools/data sources. Decouples agent code from tool implementations; turns tool integration into a plug-in ecosystem.

patterns.name: Prompt caching (as API feature)
patterns.summary: Provider-level caching of long static prompt prefixes (system prompts, tool defs, RAG context). Dramatic latency/cost reduction for repeat calls with shared prefix.
patterns.relationship_to_yan_caching: Yan's 'Caching' pattern (2023) anticipated this but was framed around semantic response caching; prompt caching is a different, complementary mechanism now native to APIs.

patterns.name: Native structured outputs
patterns.summary: API-level guarantee that model output conforms to a JSON schema (or grammar). Eliminates much of the parsing/repair code that dominated 2023 agent frameworks.

patterns.name: Long-context patterns (and the RAG-vs-long-context debate)
patterns.summary: Context windows of 1M+ tokens (Gemini 1.5+, Claude) shift some problems from 'retrieve and inject' to 'just dump everything in'. Active research on retrieval quality vs. cost vs. attention dilution.

patterns.name: Agentic RAG
patterns.summary: RAG where retrieval is performed by an agent that can plan multi-hop queries, re-rank, decide when to retrieve again, and verify before answering. Combines Yan's RAG with Anthropic's Agents.

patterns.name: GraphRAG
patterns.summary: Microsoft Research pattern (2024): build a knowledge graph from a corpus first, then retrieve and reason over graph structure rather than flat embeddings. Better for multi-hop questions over large corpora.

patterns.name: Computer use / browser use agents
patterns.summary: Models that operate a GUI directly via screenshots and synthesized mouse/keyboard actions, rather than via APIs.

patterns.name: DPO/KTO/ORPO (preference optimization beyond RLHF)
patterns.summary: Direct Preference Optimization and successors replace the reward-model + PPO pipeline of classic RLHF with simpler, more stable training. Now standard for alignment fine-tuning.

patterns.name: Context engineering (as a named discipline)
patterns.summary: Explicit pattern from 12-Factor Agents Factor 3: deliberately curate what goes into every LLM call rather than passively accumulating chat history. Includes summarization, pruning, and structured-state injection.

patterns.name: Speculative decoding
patterns.summary: Performance pattern: a small draft model proposes tokens, a large model verifies in parallel. 2-3x latency reduction. Now standard in production inference stacks (vLLM, TGI, TensorRT-LLM).

patterns_that_aged_poorly_or_were_supers.superseded_by: Native function calling — most models now ship with tool-use trained in
Claude Agent SDK, OpenAI Agents SDK, LangGraph, AutoGen, Agent Framework
